speaker: DIONYZA
text: Why, are you foolish? Can it be undone?

speaker: CLEON
text: O Dionyza, such a piece of slaughter

speaker: CLEON
text: The sun and moon ne’er looked upon!

speaker: DIONYZA
text: I think you’ll turn a child again.

speaker: CLEON
text: Were I chief lord of all this spacious world,

speaker: CLEON
text: I’d give it to undo the deed. A lady

speaker: CLEON
text: Much less in blood than virtue, yet a princess

speaker: CLEON
text: To equal any single crown o’ th’ Earth

speaker: CLEON
text: I’ the justice of compare. O villain Leonine,

speaker: CLEON
text: Whom thou hast poisoned too!

speaker: CLEON
text: If thou hadst drunk to him, ’t had been a kindness

speaker: CLEON
text: Becoming well thy face. What canst thou say

speaker: CLEON
text: When noble Pericles shall demand his child?

speaker: DIONYZA
text: That she is dead. Nurses are not the Fates.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: To foster is not ever to preserve.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: She died at night; I’ll say so. Who can cross it

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Unless you play the impious innocent

speaker: DIONYZA
text: And, for an honest attribute, cry out

speaker: DIONYZA
text: “She died by foul play!”

speaker: CLEON
text: O, go to. Well, well,

speaker: CLEON
text: Of all the faults beneath the heavens, the gods

speaker: CLEON
text: Do like this worst.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Be one of those that thinks

speaker: DIONYZA
text: The petty wrens of Tarsus will fly hence

speaker: DIONYZA
text: And open this to Pericles. I do shame

speaker: DIONYZA
text: To think of what a noble strain you are,

speaker: DIONYZA
text: And of how coward a spirit.

speaker: CLEON
text: To such proceeding

speaker: CLEON
text: Whoever but his approbation added,

speaker: CLEON
text: Though not his prime consent, he did not flow

speaker: CLEON
text: From honorable courses.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Be it so, then.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Yet none does know but you how she came dead,

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Nor none can know, Leonine being gone.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: She did distain my child and stood between

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Her and her fortunes. None would look on her,

speaker: DIONYZA
text: But cast their gazes on Marina’s face,

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Whilst ours was blurted at and held a malkin

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Not worth the time of day. It pierced me through,

speaker: DIONYZA
text: And though you call my course unnatural,

speaker: DIONYZA
text: You not your child well loving, yet I find

speaker: DIONYZA
text: It greets me as an enterprise of kindness

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Performed to your sole daughter.

speaker: CLEON
text: Heavens forgive it.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: And as for Pericles,

speaker: DIONYZA
text: What should he say? We wept after her hearse,

speaker: DIONYZA
text: And yet we mourn. Her monument is

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Almost finished, and her epitaphs

speaker: DIONYZA
text: In glitt’ring golden characters express

speaker: DIONYZA
text: A general praise to her, and care in us

speaker: DIONYZA
text: At whose expense ’tis done.

speaker: CLEON
text: Thou art like the Harpy,

speaker: CLEON
text: Which, to betray, dost with thine angel’s face

speaker: CLEON
text: Seize with thine eagle’s talons.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: You’re like one that superstitiously

speaker: DIONYZA
text: Do swear to the gods that winter kills the flies.

speaker: DIONYZA
text: But yet I know you’ll do as I advise.
